name: Candied Grapefruit Peel
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the grapefruit peel with cold water.
2. Bring to a boil and cook until tender, pouring off the water and adding fresh cold water several times.
3. Drain the peel.
4. Remove the white inner portion of the peel using a spoon.
5. Using a knife or tiny star cookie cutter, cut a few stars from the peel if desired.
6. Cut the rest of the peel into thin strips with scissors or a sharp knife.
7. Make syrup by boiling 2 cups sugar with 1 cup water.
8. Add a little red or green food coloring (a drop or two of yellow coloring with red improves the finished color).
9. Add the peel to the syrup and cook over low heat until it has a clear, candied appearance.
10. Remove the peel from the syrup in batches, allowing excess syrup to drain back into the saucepan.
11. Roll the strips separately into the remaining 1 cup sugar until well coated.
12. Place on a rack to cool.
13. Store in a tightly covered container in a cool, dry place. Keeps at least a month.
